summary: Targeted bug-fix verification for BUG A (landing/login page) and BUG B (onboarding Full Name pre-fill). BUG A verified via live Playwright run on mobile viewport: landing-screen renders, brand + tags + trust row visible, google-login-button (testID) is present/visible/tappable, auth-error-banner is NOT present on fresh page load, and tapping the button navigates to https://auth.emergentagent.com (Google OAuth flow initiated) without any JS crash or React Native red screen. BUG B verified via static code inspection of /app/frontend/app/onboarding.tsx: (1) no useEffect that calls setName from user (removed, comment at lines 63-65 explains removal), (2) name is initialised as empty string via useState('') at line 32, (3) TextInput for Full name has value={name}/onChangeText={setName} bindings and NO editable={false} prop (line 225-226 via Field component; Field never sets editable=false), (4) testID='name-input' present at line 226. Both bug fixes look correct.

react_native_issues.issue: Console warning: '"shadow*" style props are deprecated. Use "boxShadow".' — appears twice on landing load. Not blocking, but should be migrated to boxShadow in src/theme.ts or wherever shadow.card / shadow.cta are defined. Reference: /app/frontend/app/index.tsx uses shadow.card (line 131) and shadow.cta (line 197).

action_items: Optional: migrate deprecated 'shadow*' style props to 'boxShadow' in the theme (src/theme) to silence Expo/RN web warnings on the landing screen.
critical_code_review_comments: onboarding.tsx: fix looks clean — pre-fill useEffect fully removed, state is empty-string initialised, Field wrapper doesn't set editable, testID present. Nothing else to change for BUG B.
index.tsx: error banner is correctly conditional on authError only and is not rendered on fresh load. Passes intent of BUG A.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Landing OAuth entry works end-to-end up to redirect to auth.emergentagent.com. The onboarding screen itself is gated behind Google OAuth (employee role, onboarded=false) and could not be exercised in a live browser without real Google credentials — verification was static per the review request. The initial 401 on /auth/me is expected (unauthenticated bootstrap) and not a bug.
rca_of_issue: BUG A previous cause was likely the error banner (or a stale error state) rendering unconditionally; current implementation only renders it when authError is truthy — verified banner is absent on fresh load. BUG B previous cause was a useEffect keeping name in sync with user.name, which re-populated the field after every deletion; that effect has been removed, so name is now fully user-controlled from an empty initial value.
